mustVisit.name: Ahsan Manzil
mustVisit.description: Historical palace of the Nawabs of Dhaka, a stunning pink architectural marvel building situated along the banks of the Buriganga River.

mustVisit.name: National Parliament House
mustVisit.description: A magnificent architectural masterpiece designed by Louis Kahn, symbolizing the democratic framework of Bangladesh.

specialties.cuisine: World-famous Haji Biryani, Bakarkhani, and rich Mughal-influenced dishes.
specialties.crafts: Dhakai Jamdani sarees, known for their delicate and intricate weaving patterns.

guide.gettingThere: The massive Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport and intricate bus & rail networks connect Dhaka from all points.

mustVisit.name: Cox's Bazar
mustVisit.description: The world's longest unbroken natural sandy sea beach, a major tourism destination for sea lovers.

mustVisit.name: Sajek Valley
mustVisit.description: Known as the roofs of Rangamati, offering stunning vistas of clouds blending with mountain peaks.

specialties.cuisine: Famous Mezbani beef, traditional fiery curries, and rich seafood.
specialties.crafts: Tribal handloom textiles, bamboo and cane artifacts.

guide.gettingThere: Well connected by air (Shah Amanat Airport), train, and highways from Dhaka.

mustVisit.name: Somapura Mahavihara
mustVisit.description: A magnificent ancient Buddhist vihara at Paharpur, listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site.

mustVisit.name: Mahasthangarh
mustVisit.description: One of the earliest urban archaeological sites discovered in Bangladesh dating back to 3rd century BC.

specialties.cuisine: Famous for the juiciest mango varieties, Kalai Ruti, and date molasses.
specialties.crafts: Renowned for soft Rajshahi Silk production and brass metalwork.

guide.gettingThere: Excellent road and direct train networks. Domestic flights to Shah Makhdum Airport.

mustVisit.name: The Sundarbans
mustVisit.description: The largest continuous mangrove forest in the world and home to the majestic Royal Bengal Tiger.

mustVisit.name: Sixty Dome Mosque
mustVisit.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site located in Bagerhat, an impressive architectural structure of the Sultanate period.

specialties.cuisine: Chui Jhal infused meat curries and fresh tiger prawns.
specialties.crafts: Local honey, beeswax products, and traditional pottery.

guide.gettingThere: Train or bus, followed by boat networks. Domestic flights land in nearby Jessore.

mustVisit.name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
mustVisit.description: The only freshwater swamp forest in Bangladesh, enchanting travelers with its sunken trees.

mustVisit.name: Jaflong & Tea Gardens
mustVisit.description: Breathtaking views of rolling tea estates and crystal rivers carrying stones from the Himalayas.

specialties.cuisine: Exotic citrus dishes using Shatkora beef, traditional rice cakes (Pithas).
specialties.crafts: Cane furniture (Shital Pati), handcrafted tea accessories.

guide.bestTime: Rainy Season or Winter (June to February)
guide.gettingThere: Direct international and domestic flights to Osmani Airport. Scenic train rides from Dhaka.

mustVisit.name: Kuakata
mustVisit.description: The magical beach where you can observe both sunrise and sunset over the Bay of Bengal.

mustVisit.name: Floating Guava Markets
mustVisit.description: Vibrant and busy network of boats carrying fresh, green guavas down local narrow canals.

specialties.cuisine: Exceptional Hilsha fish varieties, coconut-infused recipes, and sweet yogurts.
specialties.crafts: Intricate boat building and bamboo crafts.

guide.bestTime: July for Guava Market, Winter for the beach
guide.gettingThere: Major river networks connecting launch terminals. Direct flights or comfortable highways via Padma bridge.

mustVisit.name: Kantajew Temple
mustVisit.description: A stunning late medieval Hindu temple uniquely built with exquisite terracotta art.

mustVisit.name: Tajhat Palace
mustVisit.description: A magnificent royal palace boasting grand staircases, turning into an interesting historical museum.

specialties.cuisine: Local potato and egg dishes, uniquely distinct sweetmeats.

guide.gettingThere: Connected by major North-bound highways from Dhaka and trains from kamalapur.

mustVisit.name: Muktagacha Rajbari
mustVisit.description: A grand, decaying palace hinting at the majestic lifestyles of the former Zamindars.

mustVisit.name: Garo Hills
mustVisit.description: Scenic hills lying across the Indian border populated by indigenous tribes with a rich cultural tapestry.

specialties.cuisine: The legendary Muktagachha Monda (a highly prized sweetmeat) uniquely crafted from milk.
specialties.crafts: Tribal weaves, intricate bamboo products, and local folklore arts.

guide.gettingThere: Only a comfortable few hours drive from Dhaka on dedicated highways.
